ratingScale: 0-10; 10 requires verified production evidence, green tests, operational runbooks, and no unresolved blockers.
globalBlockers: Live production is still not verified against the current repository artifact.
No final written production approval has been given.
Billion-user/day scale cannot be proven or honestly achieved on free-only GitHub and Cloudflare limits.
Real AI model orchestration is not implemented in the free-only MVP.
Legal/privacy/compliance review and abuse operations are not complete.

areas.label: Sicherheit
areas.why: Auth, CSRF, strict cookies, rate limits, delete-confirm headers, security headers, and upload checks exist, but production verification and advanced abuse protection are incomplete.
automaticImprovements: Delete confirmation headers enforced for destructive account and storage routes.
Backup, restore, and change-protection gates added to release checks.
Public security.txt and noindex checks added to artifact/live smoke tests.
blockers: Live .well-known/security.txt and static security headers are not verified on production.
No completed penetration test or third-party security review.
No malware scanning pipeline for uploaded files in the free-only architecture.
nextActions: Deploy only after written approval, then rerun live security smoke tests.
Add upload quarantine/scanning strategy that stays within the free-only constraint.
Run manual auth/session abuse tests before public launch.

areas.label: Geschwindigkeit
areas.why: The Vite app, Cloudflare edge delivery, static caching, and service worker are ready, but measured production performance is not green yet.
automaticImprovements: Static artifact checks cover manifest icons, service worker assets, and cache-sensitive public files.
Live test now verifies public root/indexing and critical static endpoints.
blockers: No current Lighthouse/WebPageTest evidence for mobile and desktop.
No production latency budget evidence for auth, API, storage, and chat flows.
Live root previously showed stale deployment behavior.
nextActions: Run Lighthouse on preview and production after deployment approval.
Add lightweight edge timing dashboards from Cloudflare logs/analytics.
Measure upload and chat time-to-first-response with realistic payloads.

areas.label: Stabilitaet
areas.why: Release gates and runbooks exist, but full E2E, live smoke, and rollback drills are not complete.
automaticImprovements: Production workflow requires release approval, freeze confirmation, rollback confirmation, and backup restore confirmation.
Backup and change protection validation scripts are included in test-all.
blockers: No fresh green full browser/mobile test matrix.
No recent restore drill evidence from production-like data.
No confirmed Cloudflare rollback drill after the latest Worker changes.
nextActions: Run Playwright desktop and mobile tests against a preview URL.
Perform one restore dry-run and attach the evidence to the release.
Run Cloudflare Worker rollback drill before production.

areas.label: Skalierung
areas.why: The system is shaped as an edge MVP, but billion-user/day scale requires paid capacity, traffic engineering, observability, regional data strategy, and model infrastructure.
automaticImprovements: Free-only data map documents Cloudflare KV and IDrive e2 boundaries.
Legacy SQL/vector targets are blocked from production policy.
blockers: Free Cloudflare/GitHub tiers cannot guarantee billion-user/day capacity.
Cloudflare KV is not a full relational or high-write chat database.
No load-test evidence beyond architecture review.
nextActions: Define realistic phase targets for MVP, beta, launch, and hyperscale.
Design paid-capable future architecture while keeping current MVP free-only.
Add synthetic load tests for the free-tier-safe MVP limits.

areas.label: SEO/AIO/GEO/AEO
areas.why: Repo contains robots, sitemap, llms, ai policy, OpenGraph, and localized shells, but live production still needs verification and public profile pages are MVP-level.
automaticImprovements: ai.txt, llms.txt, sitemap, robots, security.txt, and PWA manifest checks added.
Translate Worker now passes static AI/SEO files through instead of returning SPA HTML.
blockers: Live root previously sent X-Robots-Tag noindex,nofollow.
Live ai.txt/security.txt previously returned HTML fallback.
No Search Console/Bing verification because external paid/third-party tooling is excluded.
nextActions: After deployment approval, verify every static SEO/AIO endpoint on smyst.com.
Generate real public profile HTML only for user-approved public profiles.
Submit sitemap manually where free webmaster tools are allowed.

areas.label: KI
areas.why: The current MVP has rule-based chat behavior and AI policy files, but no production-grade model orchestration, memory retrieval, evaluation, safety system, or provider abstraction.
automaticImprovements: AI policy files document what can and cannot be indexed.
Prompt-injection risks are included in the security documentation.
blockers: No real model backend is implemented under the free-only constraint.
No evaluation harness for answer quality, safety, latency, or hallucination.
No multi-provider failover for Gemini, Claude, Grok, DeepSeek, Kimi, Manus, or Mistral.
nextActions: Define a provider-agnostic AI gateway interface without adding paid services now.
Add local/mock evaluation tests for the chat contract.
Document which future paid or user-provided model keys would be required for real AI.

areas.label: Infrastruktur
areas.why: Cloudflare Workers/Pages-style deployment, GitHub Actions gates, KV metadata, and IDrive e2 storage are defined, but live routes/secrets cannot be fully verified locally.
automaticImprovements: Release workflow now blocks production without explicit rollback and restore confirmations.
Preflight script enforces approval, freeze, backup, restore, and rollback inputs.
blockers: Cloudflare routes/secrets are not fully re-read and verified in this run.
Current live deployment appears stale compared with repository checks.
No production monitoring/alerting proof beyond free dashboard capabilities.
nextActions: Verify Cloudflare routes, Workers, Pages deployment target, and GitHub secrets before release.
Run deployment to preview first, never production without written approval.
Document exact free-tier operational limits and escalation thresholds.

areas.label: Benutzerfreundlichkeit
areas.why: The requested dark, square, compact iPhone-like chat design is implemented in the app, but final multi-device visual QA and accessibility checks are not complete.
automaticImprovements: UI has been aligned to the start-page design language with compact chat, square avatars, and bottom icon bar behavior.
Settings and profile/chat flows were consolidated around the MVP design.
blockers: No fresh visual approval from real mobile Safari/Chrome after the latest changes.
No completed accessibility audit for focus, labels, contrast, and screen readers.
No full registration/login/profile/upload journey proof from production.
nextActions: Run Browser/Playwright visual tests at 390x844, desktop, and tablet.
Add axe/accessibility smoke tests for the main flows.
Validate the GitHub login, profile, chat, and upload flow on a preview URL.

areas.label: Wartbarkeit
areas.why: Docs, manifests, runbooks, and validation scripts improved maintainability, but large UI files and legacy folders still raise maintenance cost.
automaticImprovements: Release, backup, security, storage, API, and protection reports were added.
Policy validation catches legacy paid/local production dependencies.
blockers: src/App.tsx is still a high-churn central UI file.
Legacy backend/database/frontend folders remain for reference and need continued separation.
No ownership map or changelog automation for every critical subsystem.
nextActions: Split large UI flows into smaller tested components.
Add CODEOWNERS-style ownership guidance if the repo supports it.
Keep legacy folders explicitly non-production and isolated in validation.

areas.label: Zukunftssicherheit
areas.why: The MVP now has stronger policy gates and roadmap documentation, but future hyperscale, AI, native apps, observability, and compliance need phased investment.
automaticImprovements: Final scorecard makes future gaps explicit instead of hiding them.
Free-only constraints are encoded in docs and tests.
blockers: The free-only constraint conflicts with the stated billion-user/day ambition.
Native iPhone/Android apps are scaffold-level, not app-store-ready.
No long-term data governance, model governance, or incident program is complete.
nextActions: Define phase-based architecture: free MVP, private beta, public launch, hyperscale.
Create migration path from KV-only metadata to a durable high-write data layer.
Add incident response, privacy review, and model safety governance before major launch.
